zh.lifeSummary: 當John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin於1282年5月18日出生在英國史洛普爾艾勒斯莫時，他的父親Fulk Strange16歲，母親Eleanor Giffard8歲。他於大約1295年與Isolda De Walton結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1312年2月6日在英國英格蘭史洛普爾享年29歲。

zh-hans.Ellesmere, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾艾勒斯莫
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin于1282年5月18日出生在英国史洛普爾艾勒斯莫时，他的父亲Fulk Strange16岁，母亲Eleanor Giffard8岁。他于大约1295年与Isolda De Walton结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1312年2月6日在英国英格兰史洛普爾享年29岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: VI
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 韋, see   Wei   3.

variants.name: Vy
meanings: Vietnamese: variant of   Vi  .
Cambodian: written វី, corresponding to the Khmer word meaning ‘to spread out’.

variants.name: Wei
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Li, Chao, Hong, Cheng, Jin, Ling, Ping, Yuan, Bin, Dong, Lan, Min, Chang, Chong, Chung, Houa, Pai, Sha, Yiming.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 魏, a term referring to ‘the buildings at either side of the gates of the royal palace’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wei (魏), originally the name of a fief (located in Ruicheng in Shanxi province) granted to Bi Wan, an official in the state of Jin. In 403  BC , the state of Jin was split into three, one of which was the state of Wei (魏), established by Wei Huan Zi (魏桓子). After this state was annexed by the state of Qin in 225  BC , its name was adopted by some people as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Mi (羋) by Wei Ran (魏冉), an official who lived in the state of Qin during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). (iii) borne by descendants of Wei Liao Weng (魏了翁), a scholar during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279  AD ), who changed his original name Gao (高) to his uncle's surname Wei (魏).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin was born on 18 May 1282, in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, his father, Fulk Strange, was 16 and his mother, Eleanor Giffard, was 8. He married Isolda De Walton about 1295. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 6 February 1312, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin est né le 18 mai 1282, à Ellesmere, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Fulk Strange, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Giffard, avait 8 ans. Il a épousé Isolda De Walton environ 1295. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 6 février 1312, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 29 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin am 18. Mai 1282 in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fulk Strange 16 und seine Mutter Eleanor Giffard 8. Er hat um 1295 Isolda De Walton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. Februar 1312 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin è nato il il 18 maggio 1282 nel Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Fulk Strange, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor Giffard, aveva 8 anni. Lui sposato Isolda De Walton circa 1295. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 6 febbraio 1312, nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 29 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockinが1282年5月18日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアエレスメアで生まれた時，父Fulk Strangeは16歳，母Eleanor Giffardは8歳であった。約1295年にIsolda De Waltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1312年2月6日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアで29歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin(이)가 1282년 5월 18일 영국 슈럽실르 엘레스머에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fulk Strange(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Giffard(은)는 8세였다. 그는 대략 1295년 Isolda De Walton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1312년 2월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 29세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.18 May 1282–6 Feb 1312: 18 de maio de 1282–6 de fevereiro de 1312
pt.lifeSummary: Quando John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin nasceu em 18 de maio de 1282, em Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Fulk Strange, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Giffard, tinha 8 anos. Ele casou-se com Isolda De Walton aproximadamente 1295. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 6 de fevereiro de 1312, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 29 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin родился 18 мая 1282 г. в местности эллесмир, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Fulk Strange было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Giffard, 8. Он женился на женщине по имени Isolda De Walton около 1295 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 6 февраля 1312 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 29 лет.

es.18 May 1282–6 Feb 1312: 18 de mayo de 1282–6 de febrero de 1312
es.lifeSummary: Cuando John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin nació el 18 de mayo de 1282, en Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Fulk Strange, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Eleanor Giffard, tenía 8 años. Se casó con Isolda De Walton aproximadamente 1295. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 6 de febrero de 1312, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 29 años.
